name: La Paloma
description: A beloved Elsa institution. Homemade tortillas, slow-cooked meats, and plates piled high.
whyLocalsLoveIt: The tortillas are made fresh every morning and the salsa verde has a loyal following.

name: El Ranchito
description: Classic Tex-Mex with quick service perfect for tournament schedules.
whyLocalsLoveIt: The combo plates are unbeatable value and they never keep you waiting.

name: Burger Barn
description: Smash burgers, hand-cut fries, and thick shakes. Fast enough for a halftime run.
whyLocalsLoveIt: Best burger in the valley — locals drive from neighboring towns for the double smash.

name: Lupita's Kitchen
description: Home-style cooking that feels like Sunday dinner at abuela's house.
whyLocalsLoveIt: Everything is made from scratch and the portions are generous enough to feed a whole team.

name: Taqueria Los Compadres
description: Street-style tacos from a family operation. Order by the dozen — you will.
whyLocalsLoveIt: The al pastor is legendary and you can feed a whole team for under $50.

name: Pizza Palace
description: Big pies, long tables, and a dining room that can handle a whole tournament bracket.
whyLocalsLoveIt: The only place in town with private party room space — call ahead for groups of 15+.

name: Sweet Spot
description: Paletas, soft serve, elotes, and raspas. The perfect post-game cool-down.
whyLocalsLoveIt: Homemade paletas in flavors you won't find anywhere else. Try the mango-chamoy.

name: El Mirador
description: Full-service sit-down with a menu that covers everyone — from picky eaters to hungry coaches.
whyLocalsLoveIt: The fajitas for two could feed four. Locals bring visitors here to show off Elsa's flavors.
address: 400 N Cesar Chavez Blvd, Elsa, TX 78543
